questions.question: Clustering K-Means Algorithm falls under which type of machine learning

explanations.a: Supervised learning requires labeled data, which K-means does not use.
explanations.b: K-means groups data without predefined labels.
explanations.c: Reinforcement learning is based on rewards, not clustering.
explanations.d: K-means clearly belongs to unsupervised learning.

questions.question: Which method is used to find optimal number of clusters in a dataset

explanations.a: Dunn index evaluates cluster quality, not directly optimal K.
explanations.b: Silhouette measures clustering quality but not primarily used for K selection.
explanations.c: Elbow method identifies optimal K by observing inertia reduction.
explanations.d: Elbow method is a standard approach.

explanations.a: No fixed number of iterations guarantees convergence.
explanations.b: Convergence is not fixed to 5 iterations.
explanations.d: Centroid convergence depends on data distribution and initial values.

questions.question: The distance of a point in cluster to another point in a cluster is

answers.a: Greater than its own cluster points
answers.b: Lesser than its own cluster points

explanations.a: Points inside the same cluster are closer to each other than to other clusters.
explanations.c: Distances are rarely equal in real data.
explanations.d: Cluster points are closer within the same cluster.

questions.question: The k-means clustering algorithm is an

explanations.a: Convergence is the goal, not the nature of the process.
explanations.b: K-means repeatedly updates centroids until convergence.
explanations.d: It is an iterative optimization process.

questions.question: If clusters are overlapped, this indicates

explanations.d: Overlap means clusters are not clearly formed.

explanations.a: Probability-based clustering is used in GMMs, not K-means.
explanations.b: K-means groups points based on distance similarity.
explanations.c: Density-based clustering is used in DBSCAN.

explanations.a: Centroid is not the farthest point.
explanations.c: Centroid is the average of all points in the cluster.

questions.question: If we skip feature scaling, which one will dominate

explanations.b: Missing values do not dominate distances.

questions.question: Which distance metric is used in K-means algorithm

explanations.a: Hamming distance is for categorical or binary data.
explanations.b: K-means minimizes Euclidean distance to centroids.
explanations.c: Manhattan distance is used in K-medians.
explanations.d: Cosine similarity measures angle, not distance.
